title: Steering Control Readback
summary: Manual steering input is normalized, published as steering state, and rendered back into the steering readback path.
steps: Driver wheel or bar writes Cmd::steeringAngleCmd.
Bridge command into legacy steering angle and manual readback seams.
Produce steering state, torque, and EPS state on the chassis lane.
Gateway and ADAS merge steering freshness and steeringInputNorm.
Display::steeringFrame keeps steering feedback aligned with input.
user_outcome: Steering bar, steering angle readback, and wheel feedback move together without drift.

title: Brake Stability Response
summary: Brake request, AEB pressure intent, and stability outputs are synchronized across chassis brake owners and downstream readers.
steps: Decel profile and stop request are emitted for chassis actuation.
Brake controller merges manual state, assist pressure, and stability state.
Leaf brake and stability ECUs consume the same intervention window.
Powertrain side reacts to brake and wheel speed state transitions.
Gateway and display surfaces publish brake, stability, and health readback.
user_outcome: Brake intervention feels coordinated and the driver sees consistent brake and stability state feedback.

title: Propulsion Energy Status
summary: Powertrain and energy ECUs consolidate drive, torque, battery, and charging state into the central propulsion state lane.
steps: Battery, engine, and power limits are produced into the propulsion lane.
Vehicle controller merges speed, drive, throttle, and energy conditions.
Transmission and thermal support readers react to the central propulsion state.
Gateway and ADAS consume propulsion status for visibility and control adaptation.
Display surfaces show mode, vehicle state, and support context.
user_outcome: Vehicle mode, energy state, and propulsion readiness appear as one coherent status surface.

title: Cruise Speed Support
summary: Cruise and longitudinal support state moves from support controllers into vehicle speed management and driver-facing outputs.
steps: Cruise set speed and support status are produced for vehicle control.
Vehicle controller fuses cruise state with propulsion and pedal context.
Longitudinal assist uses cruise state for warning and decel strategy.
Gateway exposes cruise freshness and selected support state.
Cluster and IVI show speed support and drive-assist context.
user_outcome: Cruise support state is readable, timely, and aligned between control and HMI.

title: Chassis Sensor Health
summary: Wheel, angle, suspension, and chassis leaf signals are collected so downstream control and visibility stay health-aware.
steps: Leaf chassis sensors publish angle, tire, and rear steer status.
Primary chassis controllers merge raw leaf state into central control state.
Vehicle and ADAS logic use chassis state for stability and warning decisions.
Gateway tracks freshness and health across the chassis domain.
Driver-visible status follows the normalized health and state lane.
user_outcome: Chassis sensing and readiness stay visible and synchronized across control and display surfaces.

title: Object Risk Fusion
summary: Radar, camera, lidar, and perception nodes are fused into one object-risk lane for ADAS warning and assist decisions.
steps: Front sensing ECUs emit object, lane, and range context.
Side radar and occupant perception enrich the risk picture.
ADAS turns multi-sensor state into warning, release, and assist candidates.
Gateway distributes the fused ADAS state toward HMI surfaces.
Display surfaces show the selected risk interpretation.
user_outcome: Object-risk interpretation is unified before any warning or assist output is shown to the driver.

title: AEB Decel Intervention
summary: AEB stop intent, decel profile, and downstream chassis response are connected as one intervention chain.
steps: Forward collision logic raises the emergency intervention need.
AEB creates stop request, decel profile, and domain health state.
Chassis intervention readers apply the shared decel profile.
Vehicle and cruise controllers react to the intervention state.
Display and gateway surfaces reflect the active emergency braking state.
user_outcome: AEB behavior reads as one intervention story instead of separate ECU reactions.

title: Lane and Surround Keeping
summary: Lane keeping, blind-spot, and surround sensing work together to support steering and warning decisions.
steps: Lane departure and blind-spot surfaces raise steering-side warnings.
Short-range radar confirms adjacent-lane occupancy and movement.
ADAS chooses whether steering-related warning or release logic should fire.
Steering controller reads the lane/surround state where needed.
Cluster and HUD show the selected lane or surround cue.
user_outcome: Lane and surround warnings appear as one steering-side assistance behavior.

title: Parking and Surround Assist
summary: Parking cameras, ultrasonic sensing, and parking controllers feed one parking-assist behavior chain.
steps: Camera and ultrasonic surfaces emit parking scene context.
Parking controllers and key modules provide maneuver readiness and state.
Parking-assist runtimes determine the active surround-assist action.
ADAS balances parking support against the wider warning context.
Display surfaces show parking or surround-assist status.
user_outcome: Parking guidance and surround assistance feel like one guided maneuver story.

title: Driver Monitor and Occupant Risk
summary: Driver state and occupant perception are fed into the warning lane before output arbitration happens.
steps: Driver and occupant monitoring raise attention and occupancy context.
ADAS combines human-state signals with the active warning picture.
Gateway exposes the chosen occupant-related warning state.
Visual surfaces show the driver or occupant-related warning meaning.
Audio alert follows the selected human-state warning severity.
user_outcome: Driver-state and occupant warnings are visible, audible, and consistent with the active context.

title: Navigation Zone Context Ingress
summary: Map and telematics context enters the runtime and becomes zone-aware alert context for downstream selection and HMI.
steps: Navigation and telematics layers raise school-zone, highway, and route context.
IVI converts map context into one runtime-visible navigation state lane.
Gateway forwards the selected navigation context to downstream domains.
ADAS uses zone context for warning prioritization and speed adjustment.
Cluster and audio surfaces express the selected zone-aware alert.
user_outcome: Zone-aware warning behavior is traceable from map context ingress to the final driver cue.

title: V2X Emergency Ingress
summary: Emergency-vehicle ingress arrives through the V2X lane and is routed into the warning decision chain.
steps: Emergency vehicle and ETA information enters through the V2X runtime owner.
Backbone and gateway surfaces expose the ingress state to other domains.
ADAS compares emergency ingress against the current alert picture.
Display surfaces present the prioritized emergency-warning context.
Audio and HUD outputs reinforce the selected emergency alert.
user_outcome: Emergency-vehicle alerts have a clear ingress point and an explainable output path.

title: Alert Arbitration and Gate
summary: Multiple warning candidates are reduced into one selected alert with explicit gateway and display semantics.
steps: ADAS reduces zone, risk, and emergency inputs into selected alert type and level.
Gateway stores why the alert was allowed, blocked, or downgraded.
IVI converts the selected alert into display and audio-ready forms.
Cluster uses the selected alert to show the final warning text and icon state.
Audio lane follows the same selected alert and base-volume priority.
user_outcome: Only one alert meaning survives to the user-facing surfaces, and the gate reason remains explainable.

title: Cluster, HUD, and Audio Output
summary: Selected warning state is rendered into cluster, HUD, and audio output without diverging message semantics.
steps: Selected alert and supporting context are packaged for visible and audible output.
Cluster output turns the alert into the main warning surface.
HUD surfaces present only the critical condensed cue.
Audio lane emits the final alert volume and sound intent.
Driver sees and hears one coherent alert story.
user_outcome: Visual and audio warning channels stay aligned instead of competing with each other.

title: Service and Access HMI
summary: Digital-key, payment, OTA, and related service surfaces are orchestrated through one HMI-facing service chain.
steps: Service-facing runtimes publish access, payment, or update state.
HMI and telematics layers convert service state into one readable lane.
Backbone services route the service state to the correct consumer surfaces.
Display surfaces expose the current service or access state.
Read or act on service state
The user sees a consistent service and access surface.
user_outcome: Service-facing screens read like one digital-access ecosystem, not isolated widgets.

title: Body Ambient Warning Output
summary: Body lighting, ambient, wiper, and exterior cues amplify the selected warning state in the comfort domain.
steps: Gateway and BCM expose selected warning meaning into the body domain.
Lighting controllers react to the selected body-facing alert cue.
Exterior comfort ECUs keep the body context visible and synchronized.
Display surfaces show the same ambient or body warning meaning.
Driver perceives a reinforced warning through body and lighting behavior.
user_outcome: Ambient and body warning outputs reinforce the selected alert instead of acting independently.

title: Access, Security, and Entry
summary: Key, access, security, and door controllers are connected into one vehicle-entry behavior chain.
steps: Digital key and passive-entry surfaces publish vehicle-access context.
Security owners determine whether the entry request is valid.
Door controllers react to the accepted entry or lock state.
BCM reflects the resulting access and door state across body surfaces.
The user sees one coherent entry and lock result.
user_outcome: Entry, lock, and security behavior can be read as one access story from request to result.

title: Comfort, Climate, and Seat
summary: Climate and seat controllers are orchestrated as one comfort-domain runtime surface with body and HMI visibility.
steps: Climate controllers expose cabin and rear climate context.
Seat ECUs provide local comfort and seat status.
BCM bridges comfort-domain state into shared body visibility.
Display surfaces expose climate and seat state to the user.
The user can interpret comfort and climate state from one surface.
user_outcome: Comfort-domain status is discoverable without hunting across separate climate and seat surfaces.

title: Validation Scenario Control
summary: Scenario command, injected state, and verdict readback are kept inside a dedicated validation control story.
steps: Scenario harness writes the exogenous test-world input seams.
Owner ECUs react to the scenario as if it were live runtime input.
Gateway and HMI surfaces show the resulting runtime behavior.
Baseline harness reads back summarized validation state.
Scenario result and readback stay explicitly separated from product ownership.
user_outcome: Validation control remains an explicit harness story instead of leaking into product ownership.

title: Backbone, Diagnostic, and Service Routing
summary: Gateway, backbone, diagnostics, and service-facing surfaces route runtime state without becoming hidden feature owners.
steps: Backbone owners surface transport-level service and routing state.
Gateway lanes expose cross-domain runtime state to the right consumers.
Diagnostic and recorder surfaces maintain the service and evidence chain.
User-facing service surfaces show the routed service outcome.
Service and diagnostic paths remain explainable from request to readback.
user_outcome: Backbone and diagnostic behavior is readable as one routing story instead of scattered support nodes.
